content.problemStatement:
You are given an odd integer NNN and two integer sequences A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã and B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã.

Your task is to reorder the elements of BBB, forming a new sequence C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã (i.e. choose a permutation P1,P2,‚Ä¶,PNP_1, P_2, \ldots, P_NP1‚Äã,P2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,PN‚Äã of the integers 111 through NNN, where Ci=BPiC_i = B_{P_i}Ci‚Äã=BPi‚Äã‚Äã for each valid iii), in such a way that the following condition holds: (A1‚äïC1)=(A2‚äïC2)=‚Ä¶=(AN‚äïCN)(A_1 \oplus C_1) = (A_2 \oplus C_2) = \ldots = (A_N \oplus C_N)(A1‚Äã‚äïC1‚Äã)=(A2‚Äã‚äïC2‚Äã)=‚Ä¶=(AN‚Äã‚äïCN‚Äã), where ‚äï\oplus‚äï denotes bitwise XOR. Find one such reordered sequence or determine that it is impossible.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
The third line contains NNN space-separated integers B1,B2,‚Ä¶,BNB_1, B_2, \ldots, B_NB1‚Äã,B2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,BN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat:
For each test case:


If there is no valid way to reorder the sequence BBB, print a single line containing the integer ‚àí1-1‚àí1.
Otherwise, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers C1,C2,‚Ä¶,CNC_1, C_2, \ldots, C_NC1‚Äã,C2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,CN‚Äã. If there are multiple solutions, you may find any one.
